plant_message: We decided on Wednesday to drop the GraphQL API. REST only going forward. The GraphQL endpoint /graphql will be deprecated in v2.5 and removed in v3.0
recall_queries: What happened to the GraphQL API?

plant_message: After benchmarking, we chose CockroachDB over Vitess for horizontal Postgres scaling. Decision logged in ADR-004. Migration planned for Q3
recall_queries: What did we choose for database scaling?

plant_message: The new analytics pipeline will be written in Go, not Python. Reason: 10x throughput improvement in benchmark. Python service will be kept for the ML inference layer only
recall_queries: What language for the analytics pipeline?

plant_message: We dropped Datadog due to cost ($40k/month). Moving to open-source stack: Prometheus + Grafana + Loki. Migration complete by end of Q2. Datadog contract ends June 30
Frontend framework changed from Vue 3 to React 19 as of v2.0 release. All new components must be React. Vue components will not receive new features, only bug fixes until end of year
recall_queries: What frontend framework do we use now?

plant_message: Unit test coverage minimum is 80% for new code, 60% for legacy code being modified. PR will be blocked by CI if coverage drops below these thresholds
We ship on Wednesdays only. Hotfixes can go out any day but require approval from two senior engineers. No releases on Fridays under any circumstances

On-call rotation is weekly, every engineer participates after 6 months tenure. PagerDuty primary alert to current on-call, secondary to tech lead. No alert fatigue: tune or delete alerts with >10 fires per week
We chose Pulumi over Terraform for new infrastructure as code. Existing Terraform stays. New projects use Pulumi TypeScript. ADR-007 documents the reasoning
recall_queries: What ADR covers the IaC choice?

plant_message: We evaluated Redis vs Memcached for the API cache layer. Chose Redis because of persistence, Lua scripting, and pub/sub. Cache TTL for user sessions: 1 hour. API responses: 5 minutes
Replaced home-grown JWT library with Auth0 after the security audit found two vulnerabilities. Auth0 tenant clawbrain.auth0.com, integration via social + enterprise SSO
recall_queries: What auth system do we use?
Why did we move to Auth0?

plant_message: Switched from Docker Compose in production to Kubernetes after the Black Friday outage. Helm charts in charts/ directory. Minimum 3 replicas for all public-facing services
recall_queries: Why did we move to k8s?
When was the Black Friday outage?

plant_message: API versioning via URL path prefix: /v1/, /v2/. Header-based versioning was rejected (harder to cache, harder to route). Old versions maintained for 12 months after deprecation notice
recall_queries: How do we version the API?
How long are old API versions supported?

plant_message: Full-text search uses PostgreSQL pg_trgm for now. Elasticsearch migration deferred until monthly active users exceed 500k. Current MAU: ~120k
recall_queries: When will we move to Elasticsearch?

plant_message: Mobile decided: React Native over Flutter. Core reason: shared code with web React codebase, smaller team context switch. iOS and Android ship simultaneously
Job queue: we chose arq (async Redis queue) over Celery for background tasks. Reason: simpler config, better asyncio integration. Celery kept for legacy email workers only
Internal service communication uses Protocol Buffers (proto3). REST external APIs return JSON. Event streaming topic schema registered in Schema Registry at https://schema-registry.prod.internal:8081
recall_queries: What data format for internal services?

plant_message: Python linting: ruff for linting and formatting (replaces flake8 + black + isort). Line length 100. TypeScript: eslint with airbnb config + prettier. Pre-commit hook enforces both
Python packages published to private PyPI at https://pypi.prod.internal. Authentication via keyring with Vault token. Package prefix must be clawbrain- (e.g. clawbrain-core, clawbrain-utils)
recall_queries: How to authenticate to internal PyPI?

plant_message: Incident severity levels: P0 (full outage, 15min SLA), P1 (partial degradation, 1hr SLA), P2 (non-critical impact, 4hr SLA), P3 (cosmetic/minor, next sprint). All P0/P1 require a postmortem within 48h

User data retention: active users retained indefinitely, deleted accounts anonymized after 30 days, audit logs kept 7 years per compliance requirement. GDPR deletion requests fulfilled within 72 hours
recall_queries: How long are audit logs kept?

plant_message: Deployment strategy: blue/green for the API service, canary for the frontend (5% → 25% → 100% over 1 hour). Automatic rollback if error rate exceeds 1% during rollout

AI feature gate: all AI-powered features require explicit user opt-in. No silent AI processing of user content. AI model used: claude-3-5-sonnet via Anthropic API, no data training opt-out required for API usage
recall_queries: Which AI model do we use?

plant_message: CODEOWNERS file enforces: backend/ owned by @backend-team, frontend/ by @frontend-team, infra/ by @devops-team, .github/ requires two DevOps approvals, db/migrations/ requires DBA sign-off
API response time budget: p50 < 50ms, p95 < 200ms, p99 < 1s for standard endpoints. File upload endpoints excluded. Violations in prod trigger PagerDuty P2 alert
